description: A taxonomy of tactics used to evade accountability in discourse.

taxonomy.name: Deflection
taxonomy.description: Shifting focus away from the core issue or actor.
examples: Pointing to a different, lesser problem.

taxonomy.name: Blame-Shifting
taxonomy.description: Assigning responsibility to another party to evade one's own.
examples: Claiming to be a victim to justify actions.

taxonomy.name: Minimization
taxonomy.description: Downplaying the severity or significance of an event or action.

taxonomy.name: Semantic Manipulation
taxonomy.description: Using specific, often misleading, language to reframe an event.
examples: 'They weren't fired, they chose to pursue other opportunities.'
Using euphemisms for harmful actions, e.g., 'enhanced interrogation'.

taxonomy.name: Obfuscation
taxonomy.description: Deliberately making information unclear, complex, or hard to understand.
examples: Releasing floods of irrelevant data (data dump).
Using excessive jargon or technical language to confuse non-experts.

taxonomy.name: Contradictory Statements
taxonomy.description: Issuing conflicting information over time or from different sources within the same organization.
examples: One official says X, another says Not X.
Changing a story after new evidence emerges.

taxonomy.name: Procedural Obstruction
taxonomy.description: Using rules, laws, or bureaucracy to delay or prevent investigation and accountability.
examples: Filing frivolous lawsuits to drain an opponent's resources.

taxonomy.name: Outright Denial
taxonomy.description: Flatly denying something happened despite evidence to the contrary.
